game_name: Star Wars Jedi Knight: Mysteries of the Sith

danger_signals.meaning: Dark side danger; corrupting influence present

danger_signals.meaning: Immediate base-level danger; strategic threat to operations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: New Republic outpost; routine Jedi training session between Katarn and Jade
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs in normal operational states; training combat scenario

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stormtrooper invasion; weapon platform asteroid bombardment; base under siege
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies attack base; evacuation blocked; Katarn defensive action required; Jade left behind

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Jade conducting solo missions for New Republic; Dromund Kaas journey; increasingly dark environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Jade undertakes assignments alone; searches for contact with missing Katarn; moves toward Sith temple

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sith temple interior; Katarn visually corrupted by dark side power; duel arena setting

environmental_storytelling.example: New Republic outpost on Altyr V serves as visual proof of Republic expansion post-Return of the Jedi; command center as strategic narrative anchor
Stormtroopers specifically signal Imperial faction presence; 20 new monster types signal expanded threat diversity beyond Dark Forces II
Dromund Kaas ancient Sith temple serves as environmental clue to Katarn's fate; dark side corruption visually manifests within temple space
Linear 14-level structure with New Republic assignments tracking player movement from safe base through increasingly hostile territories toward Sith stronghold
10 ABY timeline placement (6 years post-Return of the Jedi, 5 years post-Dark Forces II) embedded in mission briefings; Dark Empire comic series era connection

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Protective training approach; takes offensive action against Imperial threat; investigates Sith temple intel
Refuses return to light path; engages in hostile duel with apprentice; succumbs to Sith temple power
Accepts New Republic missions; conducts solo investigation; travels to Dromund Kaas; attempts redemption duel with corrupted mentor
Attack New Republic base in coordinated assault; represent primary military antagonist faction
Provide player assistance in combat; indicate safe zones or friendly territory
Expanded threat palette beyond Dark Forces II; signal escalating danger in specific locations

notes: Mysteries of the Sith uses the Sith engine with colored lighting, new textures, and improved AI to communicate world-state through visual hierarchy. The expansion emphasizes lightsaber combat and Force powers as primary world-communication mechanics. Multiplayer maps recreate iconic Star Wars film locations (Tatooine, Bespin, Death Star), using familiar environmental visuals to establish faction territory and political context. The ysalamir capture-the-flag objective carries visual/mechanical meaning: possession reduces player Force powers, visually representing struggle between Force-sensitive and non-sensitive combat paradigms. Linear 14-level single-player structure creates clear narrative progression from Republic stability through Imperial conflict to Sith corruption—world-state shift marked by environmental design, enemy placement, and NPC behavior changes.
